VINIT.justification: The subcritical stable phase representing the base ordered state before criticality is introduced.

AFWD.justification: The transformation of the system state toward a critical fixed point.

AREV.justification: The descent from complex-axis criticality back toward the real-axis Hermitian fixed point.

CLINK.justification: The sequential application of the lattice meet function between two criticality values.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-recognition of the monad as the absorbing element where meet(monad, x) = monad.

FSPLIT.justification: The branching of a state into either a standard fixed point or an exceptional point.

EVALT.justification: The successful identification of a real-axis critical point.

EVALF.justification: The identification of an exceptional point where eigenvectors coalesce.

ENGAGR.justification: A complex-axis criticality where stable and unstable behaviors are held simultaneously.

IFIX.justification: The permanent assignment of a system to a specific tier based on its criticality value.

phase_3.false_description: failure to reach criticality or divergence to supercriticality

anchor the system within the holographic boundary of the grammar
